tools.name: ChatGPT
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: On free/Plus consumer ChatGPT, conversations are used to improve models by default unless you turn off 'Improve the model for everyone' in data controls.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: OpenAI does not train on inputs or outputs from ChatGPT Team, ChatGPT Enterprise, or the API platform by default.
tools.retention: Consumer chats retained until deleted (~30 days after deletion); ChatGPT Enterprise/Team customer-configurable retention; API logs default ~30 days and Zero Data Retention available for eligible API endpoints/in-region projects.
tools.notable_risk: Consumer/free ChatGPT uses your chats for training unless you proactively disable it, so staff pasting work data into personal accounts can leak it into model improvement.

sources.claim: Business data (Team/Enterprise/API) not used for training by default; consumer trains unless opted out
How consumer data is used to improve models and how to opt out
Certifications: SOC 2 Type 2, ISO 27001, ISO 42001, EU data residency, HIPAA BAA via API
HIPAA BAA availability for API Services
EU data residency for ChatGPT Enterprise/Edu/API

tools.name: Claude
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: Since the Aug 2025 consumer terms update, Free/Pro/Max chats and coding sessions are used to train models unless the user opts out, with up to 5-year retention if left on.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: Claude for Work (Team/Enterprise), Claude for Education, Claude Gov, and the API run under Commercial Terms and are not used for model training.
tools.retention: Consumer: 30 days if training off, up to 5 years if training on; API inputs/outputs deleted after ~7 days and never used for training; safety-flagged content may be retained up to 2 years regardless of opt-out.
tools.notable_risk: Even when a consumer opts out of training, conversations flagged for safety review can still be retained up to two years and used to improve models without notifying the user.

sources.claim: Aug 2025 consumer terms: Free/Pro/Max train unless opted out, 5-year retention; commercial (Team/Enterprise/API) excluded
Certifications: ISO 42001 for responsible AI
Certifications obtained (SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, ISO 42001) and HIPAA BAA availability

tools.name: Google Gemini
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: On consumer Gemini Apps (personal Google accounts), activity is used to improve Google services and a subset is human-reviewed unless Gemini Apps Activity is turned off.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: Google Workspace with Gemini does not use customer content to train models outside the customer's domain and content is not human-reviewed.
tools.retention: Consumer: default up to 18 months (configurable 3-36 months) tied to Gemini Apps Activity, and human-reviewed chats disconnected from the account are kept up to 3 years; Workspace data follows the customer's standard Workspace retention/Vault.
tools.notable_risk: Personal Gemini accounts have human reviewers reading a sample of chats (kept up to 3 years even after you delete activity), so confidential work content typed into a personal account can be seen by reviewers.

sources.claim: Consumer Gemini Apps human review and training; Workspace data not trained/reviewed
Workspace Gemini does not use customer data to train models outside the domain
HIPAA BAA and additional ISO certifications (incl. ISO 42001) for the Gemini app
SOC compliance for Gemini

tools.name: Microsoft 365 Copilot
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: Microsoft 365 Copilot is a paid commercial add-on; with enterprise data protection, prompts/responses are not used to train foundation models.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: Prompts, responses, and Microsoft Graph data accessed by Microsoft 365 Copilot are not used to train the foundation LLMs.
tools.retention: Copilot interaction history stored in the customer's tenant (Substrate/Exchange) and governed by the tenant's Microsoft 365 retention, eDiscovery, and Purview policies; not retained for training.
tools.notable_risk: Copilot inherits the user's existing permissions, so over-shared SharePoint/OneDrive content becomes far easier for employees to surface. That is an oversharing and governance risk rather than a training one.

sources.claim: Prompts, responses, and Graph data not used to train foundation LLMs
GDPR, EU Data Boundary, ISO/IEC 27018 compliance and DPA coverage
Microsoft ISO/IEC 42001 AI management system certification

tools.name: GitHub Copilot
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: On free/Pro/Pro+ individual plans, prompts and code snippets may be used to improve models unless the user disables it in Copilot settings.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: GitHub does not use Copilot Business or Enterprise prompts/suggestions to train models (including third-party models such as Anthropic/Google).
tools.retention: Business/Enterprise: prompts and suggestions are not retained (transient); user engagement data kept ~24 months. Individual: prompts retained ~28 days for the code-completion service.
tools.notable_risk: On individual (free/Pro) plans, code snippets can be retained and used for model improvement unless opted out, so developers on personal accounts may expose proprietary code.

sources.claim: Copilot Business/Enterprise data not used to train models incl. third-party models; individual opt-out
Free/Pro/Pro+ Privacy Statement update on Copilot data use for model training
Compliance: SOC 2 Type 2, ISO 27001 (GitHub Trust Center)

tools.name: Notion AI
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: Notion states that by default neither it nor its AI subprocessors use Customer Data to train any models, across all plan tiers.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: Notion has contractual agreements prohibiting its AI subprocessors from training on Customer Data, and your use of Notion AI grants Notion no license to train ML models on your data.
tools.retention: LLM providers retain Customer Data 30 days or fewer on non-Enterprise plans; Enterprise plans default to zero data retention with the LLM providers.
tools.notable_risk: Some AI features can optionally enable data-retaining LLMs via workspace settings, so an admin must confirm the workspace stays on zero/short-retention configurations.

sources.claim: By default Notion and its AI subprocessors do not use Customer Data to train any models; contractual agreements prohibit it.
Notion AI is in scope of SOC 2 Type 2 report and ISO 27001 certification; GDPR and HIPAA (via zero-retention LLM APIs) supported.
Non-Enterprise plans: LLM providers retain Customer Data 30 days or fewer; Enterprise default zero retention.

tools.name: Otter.ai
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: Otter uses a proprietary method to de-identify user data and trains its own models on it by default, with opt-out controls reportedly available in account data settings.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: Otter de-identifies and trains on user data by default (third-party AI service providers do not train on it); enterprise customers act as data controllers and can configure controls.
tools.retention: Conversations stored on Otter's cloud (AWS S3, AES-256) until deleted; deleted items auto-purge from trash after 30 days; admins can set custom auto-deletion durations.
tools.notable_risk: Otter trains its own models on de-identified user content by default and its meeting assistant can auto-join calendar meetings, raising consent and surveillance concerns (it is the subject of a wiretap/consent class action).

sources.claim: Otter uses a proprietary method to de-identify user data before training its own models; third-party AI providers do not train on customer data.
Otter holds a SOC 2 Type 2 report and follows GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA, and ISO 27001/2 frameworks.
Otter.ai is subject to a class-action lawsuit over recording consent / two-party consent.

tools.name: Perplexity
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: On consumer/free/Pro accounts an 'AI data retention' setting is on by default and can be used to improve models; logged-in users can opt out in Settings > Account, but logged-out users cannot.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: Perplexity Enterprise Pro and the Sonar API operate under Zero Data Retention and state customer/enterprise data is never used to train models.
tools.retention: Consumer: data retained unless 'AI data retention' is disabled; Enterprise Pro offers configurable retention and a Zero Data Retention mode; Sonar API retains no request data.
tools.notable_risk: On consumer plans data is used to improve models unless you turn off the default-on 'AI data retention' toggle, and reporting alleges Perplexity shared conversational data with ad/tracking platforms (incl. in Incognito).

sources.claim: Sonar API / Enterprise: Perplexity does not use customer data to train models and maintains Zero Data Retention; SOC 2 Type II attested.
Consumer users can opt out of AI training via the 'AI data retention' toggle in account settings; logged-out users cannot opt out.
Enterprise Pro provides SSO/SAML (SAML 2.0, OIDC), SOC 2 Type 2, audit logs and Zero Data Retention; enterprise data never used to train models.

tools.name: Grammarly
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: Grammarly does not let LLM providers train on user content, but uses aggregated, de-identified samples for product improvement and training unless the user opts out via account controls.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: Grammarly Business/Enterprise content is not used to train models and EU/UK user content is explicitly excluded from model training.
tools.notable_risk: On free/Premium consumer tiers Grammarly uses de-identified content for product improvement/training unless you opt out, and HIPAA coverage requires an Enterprise BAA (not available on Free/Premium/Business).

sources.claim: Grammarly does not allow LLM providers to train on user content, but uses aggregated de-identified samples for product improvement/training unless users opt out.
Grammarly holds SOC 2 Type 2 and ISO 27001/27002; is HIPAA-compliant only with an Enterprise BAA.

tools.name: Fireflies.ai
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: Fireflies states meeting data is never used for AI model training and operates a 0-day retention arrangement with its ASR/LLM vendors (incl. signed BAAs) so providers don't retain or train on data.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: Same policy applies to paid/enterprise tiers; raw/derived data from Google Workspace APIs is not used to train foundation models and vendor BAAs prevent third-party training.
tools.retention: Meeting data retained by default while the account is active; deleting a meeting purges it immediately, and deleting an account removes all data within 30 days; Enterprise can enforce retention via the Rules Engine.
tools.notable_risk: Fireflies' bot auto-joins calendar meetings to record/transcribe, creating consent exposure, and HIPAA-grade Private Storage plus EU data hosting are gated to Enterprise deals.

sources.claim: 0-day retention ensures meeting data is never used for AI model training; Fireflies signed BAAs with OpenAI and other ASR vendors so no data is stored or used to train their algorithms.
Fireflies is SOC 2 Type II (audited annually), GDPR and HIPAA compliant and aligns with ISO 27001.
SSO/SAML and EU data hosting / Private Storage are available on the Enterprise plan; DPA uses SCCs for EU/UK/Swiss transfers.

tools.name: Zoom AI Companion
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: Zoom states it does not use any customer audio, video, chat, screen sharing, attachments, or other communications-like customer content to train its own or third-party AI models, and this applies across all account tiers.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: Same blanket no-training commitment applies to paid/enterprise content; no customer content is used to train Zoom's or third-party models.
tools.retention: No single universal period; AI Companion outputs follow account/group/user retention settings. AI Companion inputs (e.g. the transcript used to generate a summary) are retained up to ~30 days for support/debugging; local file uploads to AI Companion web are stored up to 7 days; third-party model providers operate under zero-data-retention policies.
tools.enterprise_plan: AI Companion (included with eligible paid Zoom Workplace accounts: Pro/Business/Enterprise)
tools.notable_risk: AI Companion must process meeting audio/transcripts to function, and those inputs can be retained up to ~30 days for support/debugging, so disabling or admin-scoping the feature is the only way to keep sensitive conversations out of that processing pipeline.

sources.claim: Zoom does not use customer audio, video, chat, screen sharing, attachments, or other communications-like customer content to train its own or third-party AI models (across all tiers).
AI Companion whitepaper confirms ISO 27001, ISO 27701, ISO 27017/18 and SOC 2 in scope; GDPR DPA available; EU data residency offered.
Zoom Global DPA (GDPR data processing agreement).
AI Companion inputs retained up to 30 days for support/debugging; data-handling details.

tools.name: Slack AI
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: Slack states Customer Data is never used to train third-party LLMs, and generative AI models are not developed using Customer Data; this applies across tiers (Slack AI is not offered on free/consumer tiers).
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: For paid/enterprise use, Slack AI uses off-the-shelf LLMs hosted within Slack's own AWS trust boundary that do not retain or train on Customer Data, using RAG to send only inference-time data.
tools.retention: Slack AI responses (summaries, search answers) are ephemeral and not stored on servers; recaps are retained ~90 days; workflow-generated channel summaries follow the workspace's configured data retention/message-retention settings.
tools.enterprise_plan: Enterprise Grid (Slack AI is a paid add-on on paid plans; admins can scope AI features)
tools.notable_risk: The 'no training' guarantee covers generative LLMs, but Slack's non-generative machine-learning features have historically processed customer messages on an opt-out (not opt-in) basis, so admins must proactively review AI/ML data settings rather than assume default protection.

sources.claim: Customer Data is never used to train third-party LLMs; AI in Slack uses off-the-shelf LLMs hosted on Slack's cloud provider (AWS) within Slack's trust boundary and does not retain Customer Data after inference.
Slack AI security: customer data is never used to train third-party LLMs; admins/owners control which AI features members can use; AI responses are ephemeral while recaps are retained ~90 days.
Slack holds enterprise security and compliance certifications including SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR DPA availability, HIPAA BAA support, and EU data residency options.

tools.name: Cursor
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: For free/Pro individual users Privacy Mode is opt-in (off by default), and with it off Cursor states it 'may use and store codebase data, prompts, editor actions, code snippets, and other code data and actions to improve our AI features and train our models.'
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: Privacy Mode is enforced/on by default for Team and Enterprise plans, so code is not used for training by Cursor or its model-provider subprocessors.
tools.retention: With Privacy Mode enabled, Cursor maintains Zero Data Retention (ZDR) agreements with inference subprocessors so prompts/completions are not retained; without Privacy Mode, code data may be stored to improve and train models (specific retention windows not published).
tools.notable_risk: On the free/Pro tier Privacy Mode is opt-in and OFF by default, so an at-work user who does not enable it has their code, prompts and editor actions stored and used to train Cursor's models.

sources.claim: Privacy Mode opt-in for free/Pro and training on code when Privacy Mode is off; Privacy Mode enforced/default-on for Teams/Enterprise
SOC 2 Type II attestation report available on request; Privacy Mode available to free/Pro and enforceable by team/enterprise admins
ISO 27001 certification and GDPR-compliant DPA; Privacy Mode enforced by default for Teams and Enterprise; ZDR with subprocessors

tools.name: DeepSeek
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: DeepSeek's privacy policy states it uses user inputs to train and improve its machine-learning models by default, while granting users a right to opt out of having their personal data used for training.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: DeepSeek publishes no separate business/API privacy terms or DPA confirming a no-training default for paid API usage; third-party sources claim paid API data is excluded from training, but this is unverified against official vendor documentation.
tools.retention: No fixed period stated; personal data is retained 'for as long as necessary to provide our Services,' e.g. for as long as the user maintains an account, per the privacy policy.
tools.notable_risk: The hosted DeepSeek app and API store user prompts and personal data on servers in the People's Republic of China (subject to Chinese law) and use inputs to train models by default. This is a major governance risk that has led to government bans and restrictions in Italy, South Korea, Australia, and multiple US federal agencies.

sources.claim: User data is collected, processed and stored in the People's Republic of China and inputs are used to train and improve DeepSeek's models, with a stated right to opt out of training.

No verifiable SOC 2 / ISO 27001 / ISO 42001 certification, DPA, BAA, or EU data residency for the hosted DeepSeek service (third-party certification claims are conflicting and unverified against official sources).

DeepSeek has been banned or restricted by Italy (Jan 2025), South Korea, Australia (government devices), and US federal agencies (e.g. NASA, US Navy) over data-handling and China data-access concerns.

tools.name: Midjourney
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: Midjourney's Terms of Service grant it a broad license to reproduce and prepare derivative works of submitted text/image prompts and generated assets, which it uses to train and improve its models by default.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: Paid Pro/Mega users are also subject to model training; Stealth Mode only keeps generations out of the public gallery and Midjourney still retains the license to use them for service improvement.
tools.retention: Not specified in Midjourney's published Terms of Service or help docs; no defined retention or deletion timeline was found, and Midjourney retains a perpetual license to submitted content.
tools.notable_risk: All prompts and generated images are public by default and licensed for model training, and Stealth Mode (Pro/Mega only) merely hides outputs from the public gallery without exempting them from training, so confidential work-related content should not be used.

sources.claim: Midjourney's ToS grants a broad license to reproduce/prepare derivative works of prompts and images (training), content is public and remixable by default, and Stealth Mode only prevents publishing while Midjourney still retains the license to use content for service improvement.
Third-party AI vendor risk profile: SOC 2/ISO/GDPR DPA/HIPAA/EU residency/SSO status unknown/unverified; no sequestered (VPC/on-prem) access and no enterprise SLAs available.
Stealth Mode is available only on Pro/Mega plans and keeps generations private from the public gallery.

tools.name: Jasper
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: Jasper has no free tier (trial only); its trust page states company data and outputs are never used to train the underlying third-party LLMs, so the default trial/starter behavior is no training.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: Jasper states 'Your company data and outputs are never used to train third-party LLMs,' applying to paid Team/Business/Enterprise tiers by default.
tools.notable_risk: Jasper relies on third-party LLM providers (e.g., OpenAI/Anthropic) under no-training API agreements, so an at-work user's confidential prompts still transit external model vendors and trust rests on contractual rather than self-hosted controls.

sources.claim: Company data and outputs are never used to train third-party LLMs
SOC 2, GDPR, CCPA certifications and downloadable SOC 2 report
Security program aligned to AICPA Trust Services Principles; SSO, audit logs, granular permissions; encryption (TLS in transit, AES-256 at rest); non-retention of user data for training

tools.name: Gamma
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: On Free/Plus/Pro/Ultra individual plans, Gamma uses de-identified, anonymized content to improve its AI features by default, but users can disable this in account settings at any time.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: For all Team and Business workspaces, content is automatically and permanently excluded from AI training (the setting is locked and cannot be enabled).
tools.retention: Decks/content retained while the account is active; deleted content follows standard SaaS deletion. Specific quantified retention windows not published; deletion available on account closure.
tools.notable_risk: On individual Free/Plus plans your presentation content is fed into AI model improvement by default, so an at-work user must manually opt out (or move to a Team/Business workspace) to keep client material out of training.

sources.claim: Free/Plus default-on training with opt-out; Team/Business locked off
SOC 2 Type II compliant
Data Processing Addendum (GDPR)

tools.name: ElevenLabs
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: By default ElevenLabs uses voice/usage data to improve its models for consumer accounts; users can disable the 'Improve the models for everyone' toggle under Data use in account settings.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: ElevenLabs states it does not train on Enterprise customer data (beyond what is needed to provide the service), and third-party LLM providers are contractually barred from training on customer content.
tools.retention: Default retention to provide the service; optional enterprise Zero Retention Mode immediately deletes request/response data on completion. Uploaded voice recordings deleted after a long inactivity window; voice clones persist until deleted.
tools.notable_risk: On the default consumer tier your submitted audio/voice data is used to improve models unless you flip the opt-out toggle, and voice cloning carries real consent/likeness-misuse exposure if someone uploads a voice they aren't authorized to clone.

sources.claim: Consumer default model-improvement use with opt-out toggle
Enterprise no-training, Zero Retention Mode
SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, ISO 42001, HIPAA, GDPR certifications
EU data residency (Enterprise)

tools.name: Synthesia
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: Synthesia states it does not use customer inputs or outputs to pre-train its AI components; any fine-tuning on customer data only happens on the customer's written instructions.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: Same policy applies across paid/business tiers: customer data is not used to pre-train models, and Synthesia acts as a GDPR processor on the customer's behalf.
tools.retention: Customer data processed as a GDPR processor under the customer agreement; videos/data retained while the account is active and deleted on customer instruction/termination. Specific quantified windows governed by the agreement.
tools.notable_risk: The main governance gotcha is consent provenance for AI avatars and voices. Synthesia requires documented consent for any likeness, so an at-work user must ensure they have rights to any face or voice they upload to create a custom avatar.

sources.claim: Does not pre-train AI on customer inputs/outputs; fine-tuning only on customer instruction
SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, ISO 27701, ISO 42001 (world-first), GDPR
World's first ISO 42001-compliant AI video company

tools.name: Canva Magic Studio
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: Disputed: Canva officially states training is opt-in (off by default) for Free/Pro, but multiple third-party reports describe AI-training toggles enabled by default that users must turn off; treat as opt-out and verify in current account privacy settings.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: For Teams, Business, Enterprise and Education accounts Canva states content is never used for AI training and cannot be opted in.
tools.retention: Designs/content retained while the account is active; deletion follows standard account-deletion flow. Specific quantified retention windows not separately published for Magic Studio.
tools.notable_risk: There is a genuine discrepancy between Canva's stated opt-in default and third-party reports of training toggles being on by default for Free/Pro accounts, so an at-work individual user should explicitly verify and disable both 'usage' and 'content' AI-training toggles in privacy settings.

sources.claim: Teams/Business/Enterprise content never used for training; consumer training controlled via privacy preferences (disputed default)
Magic Studio safe-use / training-consent guidance
Canva Shield: safe, fair, secure AI (SOC 2 / ISO 27001 / GDPR posture)

tools.name: Adobe Firefly
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: Adobe states it does not use customer content to train the Firefly models; Firefly's base models are trained on licensed Adobe Stock, openly licensed, and public-domain content.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: Adobe explicitly does not train Firefly on enterprise/business customer content, and markets Firefly outputs as 'commercially safe' with IP indemnification for enterprise customers.
tools.retention: Content stored per Creative Cloud / enterprise agreement; enterprise customer content not used for training and governed by the Adobe enterprise data-protection terms with deletion on request/termination.
tools.enterprise_plan: Adobe Firefly for enterprise / Creative Cloud for enterprise
tools.notable_risk: The bigger governance question is output provenance and indemnity scope rather than training. Firefly is 'commercially safe' and IP-indemnified mainly for enterprise customers, so individual and free users should not assume the same legal coverage for generated assets.

sources.claim: Adobe does not use customer content to train Firefly models; commercially safe approach
Adobe does not use customer content to train Firefly (Adobe Express enterprise security overview)
SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA-ready certifications

tools.name: Read AI
tools.trains_on_personal_data_note: Read AI is opt-out of training by default: meeting content is not used to improve the product unless a user explicitly opts in via a checkbox in settings.
tools.trains_on_business_data_note: Default is opt-out across tiers; HIPAA-locked workspaces make sharing data for training permanently impossible, and training only occurs on explicit user opt-in.
tools.retention: User-configurable: users choose whether audio/video is saved for playback; recordings/transcripts can be processed, summarized, then deleted, and HIPAA workspaces support no-retention configurations.
tools.notable_risk: The biggest gotcha is the auto-join bot behaviour. Read can be invited by any participant and join meetings automatically, so sensitive conversations may be silently recorded or transcribed unless hosts and participants actively remove it.

sources.claim: Default opt-out of training; opt-in only via settings checkbox
SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA, GDPR compliant; privacy-first notetaker
Auto-join behaviour, host approval, opt-out controls
